=== PAGE: https://iccms.org/visit.html ===
Link: Discover Hangzhou
Title: 18th ICCMS 2026-Computer Modeling and Simulation 第十八届计算机建模与仿真国际会议
18th ICCMS 2026-Computer Modeling and Simulation 第十八届计算机建模与仿真国际会议
iccms_contact@163.com
+86-18302820449
Discover Hangzhou
Hangzhou, 
									the
																		capital of Zhejiang Province and Song 
									Dynasty capital, is known as 'Heaven on 
									Earth', with its stunning natural beauty and 
									rich cultural heritage. It was described by 
									Marco Polo as "the finest and most splendid 
									city in the world".
									Hangzhou is the political, economic, 
									cultural, educational, transportation, and 
									financial center of Zhejiang Province. The 
									2022 Asian Games was held in Hangzhou.
Where Hangzhou Is Located
Hangzhou is on the east coast of China, 
									south of the Yangtze River Delta and about 
									170 kilometers (105 miles) south of 
									Shanghai. By bullet train, Hangzhou is about 
									1 hour from Shanghai and 1.5 hours from 
									Suzhou.
The Features 
									of Hangzhou
▪
Hangzhou is
one of the Seven Ancient Capital Cities
, 
									with a history of about 2,500 years. Take a 
									cruise to explore the World Heritage Site - 
									the Grand Canal.
▪
Hangzhou boasts
enchanting natural scenery
.
West Lake
, the symbol of Hangzhou, is 
									considered to be one of the most beautiful 
									sights in China.
▪
Hangzhou offers
a variety of traditional cultures
, 
									such as tea, traditional Chinese medicine, 
									and Chinese silk.
▪
Hangzhou has a
highly developed transportation network
. 
									It is almost the only city in China 
									following 'pedestrian first' laws.
Hangzhou Is a 
									Leisurely City
Hangzhou is a special city. It is developing 
									at a high speed, while the lifestyle in 
									Hangzhou is leisurely. Compared to Shanghai, 
									the pace of life there is slow.
When traveling in Hangzhou, you can have a 
									stroll by West Lake, take a boat cruise, 
									feed the goldfish in the lake, and enjoy the 
									beautiful scenery. Then have a delicious 
									afternoon tea by the lake and chat with your 
									guide, learning about the story and history 
									of Hangzhou.
If you want to feel the classical charm of 
									Hangzhou, you can pay a visit to some water 
									towns. There are old buildings, boats, 
									rivers, ancient bridges, and wells. 
									Everything there is peaceful and beautiful.
If you want to know more about the tea 
									culture of Hangzhou, you can go to Meijiawu 
									Tea Plantation for an idyllic tour. Pick tea 
									leaves with a local tea farmer, watch a 
									Chinese tea ceremony, and learn about 
									Chinese tea culture.
5 Highlights 
									of Hangzhou
Hangzhou is a 
									perfect combination of history and 
									modernity, nature and culture. When you 
									visit Hangzhou, don't miss the following 
									highlights.
1. West Lake - the Legend of Hangzhou
West Lake is a symbol of Hangzhou. It is 
									famous for its stunning scenery and various 
									historical sites. It was selected as one of 
									the "12 superb sunset spots around the 
									world" by CNN.
West Lake
is gorgeous all year round. The spring 
									flowers, the summer lotuses, the autumn 
									moon, and the winter snow all make it very 
									charming.
2. Water Towns 
									Near Hangzhou
Wuzhen and Nanxun are the two most popular 
									water towns near Hangzhou.
Wuzhen
is one of the top six water towns in 
									China. Taking a gondola along the ancient 
									canal should not be missed and the nighttime 
									scenery in Wuzhen is impressive.
Nanxun
is not as famous as Wuzhen. It is thanks to 
									this that Nanxun is the most original and 
									picturesque town, filled with a tranquil 
									ambiance.
3. The Grand 
									Canal - World Heritage Site
The Grand Canal was dug out in 486 BC and 
									has a history of more than 2,500 years. It 
									runs from Beijing in the north to Hangzhou 
									in the south, stretching for 1,747 
									kilometers.
It played a great role in the economic and 
									cultural development, and with exchanges 
									between the north and south of China. It was 
									a great project created by the ancient 
									Chinese and is now one of the symbols of 
									Chinese culture.
4. Dragon Well Tea
Hangzhou's unique Dragon Well tea (Longjing 
									Tea) plays an important role among the 
									varieties of Chinese green tea. It is 
									produced mostly by hand, and is honored as 
									China's Famous Tea due to its high quality.
5. Hangzhou Silk
Hangzhou is regarded as the cradle of the 
									Chinese silk culture and production. With a 
									history of over 2,500 years, Hangzhou silk 
									is renowned for its gentle texture, gorgeous 
									colors, and wide varieties.
Convenient Transportation Network
Hangzhou is easily accessible by high-speed 
									trains and flights from main cities, such as 
									Beijing, Shanghai, Suzhou, Xi'an, Chengdu, 
									and Hong Kong.
From Hangzhou, the direct bus is the most 
									convenient way to get to nearby water towns. 
									A private tour with a private vehicle is the 
									best hassle-free option to avoid the traffic 
									congestion and help you maximize your time.
See more on
Hangzhou transportation
.
Emergency Phone 
									Numbers
▪ 110 for police
▪ 112 for traffic police (emergency only)
▪ 119 for fire services
▪ 120 for medical assistance or an ambulance
If you encounter a really complex situation, 
									call 110 first.

Visa Information
Chinese 
						Visa
Conference participants may need a Chinese visa to enter 
						China. Specifically, those who are not Chinese citizens, 
						except passport holders from Singapore, Brunei, and 
						Japan, need a Chinese visa. Participants can apply for a 
						visa at the Chinese embassy or consulate in the region 
						in which they live. To avoid uncertainty, participants 
						will be advised to apply for a visa as early as 
						possible. It is recommended that they apply for a 
						Chinese visa at least 1 month in advance.
For most attendees, it is easier to apply for a Tourist 
						("L") visa; an invitation letter is NOT required for 
						obtaining a Tourist visa.
Necessary materials to apply 
						for Chinese Visa
Â»A passport which is valid for at least six months 
						following the date of submission, with an entire blank 
						page available for the visa;
Â»One Visa Application Form of the People's Republic of 
						China, which is filled clearly with true, authentic and 
						complete information and signed by the applicant;
Â»One recent, 2 inch full-faced and bareheaded passport 
						photo (the daily life photo, photo copy or digital photo 
						printed on the ordinary paper shall not be accepted);
Â»Other related application materials according to 
						different purposes for your trip to China;
Â»Other materials related to the visa application, deemed 
						as necessary by the visa officer.
* For more information, please contact the local Chinese 
						Embassy in your country.
Invitation Letters
1. The invitation letter will be issued after the 
						successful registration.
2. Your name must be listed exactly as it appears on 
						your passport. Any differences between the name on your 
						passport and the name on your invitation letter or other 
						documentation could lead to a delay and/or denial of 
						your visa.
3. Please note ICCMS Conferences and the organizing 
						committee of the conference are not authorized to assist 
						with the VISA process beyond providing the Invitation 
						Letter issued by ICCMS Conferences and conference 
						Committee Board. Should your application be denied, 
						ICCMS Conferences and the organizing committee of the 
						conference cannot change the decision of the Ministry of 
						Foreign Affairs, nor will ICCMS Conferences and the 
						organizing committee of the conference engage in 
						discussion or correspondence with the MOFA or the 
						Embassy on behalf of the applicant. The registration fee 
						cannot be refunded when the VISA application of 
						individual being denied.
4. It takes around one month for the Embassy to process 
						the VISA application, please register as early as you 
						can to make sure you have enough time.

Keynote Speakers
Prof. Seifedine Kadry (IET Fellow)
Lebanese American University, USA
Seifedine Kadry is a 
									professor of Data Science at Lebanese 
									American university, USA. He holds a HdR 
									(Habilitation Degree) in Engineering 
									Sciences from Rouen University, France 
									(2017) and a Ph.D. in Engineering Sciences 
									from Blaise-Pascal University, France 
									(2007). He also earned an M.S. in Applied 
									Mathematics and Computer Science from EPFL 
									Lausanne, Switzerland (2002) and a B.S. in 
									Computer Science and Applied Mathematics 
									from the Lebanese University (1999). 
									Previously, he was a professor at the 
									Department of Mathematics and Computer 
									Science at Beirut Arab University and the 
									founder and director of the Data Science 
									Center there. He also held academic 
									positions at the American University of the 
									Middle East, Kuwait, and Lebanese 
									University, Lebanon.

ICCMS 2025
ICCMS 2025
June 13-15, 2025 |
Zhuhai, China
The 17th International Conference on Computer Modeling 
						and Simulation (ICCMS 2025) was held in Zhuhai, China 
						during June 13-15, 2025 successfully. ICCMS 2025 is 
						co-organized by Intelligent IoT System Modeling and 
						Simulation Committee of China Simulation Federation, 
						China and Beihang University, China, co-hosted by Jinan 
						University (Zhuhai Campus), China; The Hong Kong 
						Polytechnic University, Hong Kong, China and Hangzhou 
						International Innovation Institute of Beihang 
						University, China. The conference featured 14 thematic 
						sub-forums covering hot topics such as intelligent 
						modeling and simulation, modeling and optimization 
						calculation in human-computer collaboration and 
						interactive design, Ssructural design, functional 
						simulation and simulation analysis in mechanical and 
						manufacturing systems.

						Singapore.

ICCMS 2024
June 21-23, 2024 |
Virtual Conference (Dalian, China)
The 16th International Conference on Computer Modeling and Simulation 
						(ICCMS 2024) was held virtually during 
						June 21-23, 2024. ICCMS 
						2024 was organized by Dalian University of Technology, 
						China, co-organized by Chongqing University of Posts and 
						Telecommunications, China and Heilongjiang Province Key 
						Laboratory of Laser Spectroscopy Technology and 
						Application, China.
Three keynote speakers and one invited speaker shared 
						with us their research works. They are Prof. Alejandro F 
						Frangi (IEEE Fellow & SPIE Fellow), The University of 
						Manchester, UK; Prof. Xiao Song, Beihang University, 
						China; Prof. Xiangjian He, University of Nottingham 
						Ningbo China, China.
ICCMS 2024 provided a professional exposure and exchange 
						platform for academicians, researchers, scientists, 
						engineers and technologists internationally, who focus 
						on the field of Computer Modeling and Simulation, to 
						share their researches and collaborate through 
						conference discussions.

ICCMS 2021
ICCMS 2021
JUNE 25-27, 2021 |
Virtual Conference (Melbourne, Australia)
The 13th International Conference on Computer Modeling 
						and Simulation (ICCMS 2021) has been held online during 
						June 25-27, 2021. The conference had initially been 
						scheduled to be held in Melbourne, Australia, but due to 
						the outbreak of the COVID pandemic, the conference was 
						switched to the virtual conference form. ICCMS 2021 was 
						co-organized by Swinburne University of Technology, 
						Australia and University of Wollongong, Australia, and 
						technically supported by Chongqing University of Post 
						and Telecommunications, China.
Three renowned keynote speakers shared with us their 
						research works. They are Prof. Ming C. Lin (ACM Fellow, 
						IEEE Fellow) from University of Maryland, College Park, 
						USA; Prof. Makoto Iwasaki (IEEE Fellow) from Nagoya 
						Institute of Technology, Japan; Prof. Marimuthu 
						Palaniswami (IEEE Fellow) from The University of 
						Melbourne, Australia. Besides, there were three invited 
						speakers shared their latest research results with us.
Authors from Japan, Australia, Austria, The Netherlands, 
						China, etc. were divided into 7 parallel sessions. Their 
						presentations covered topics such as Computer and 
						Information Technology, Mechanical and Control 
						Engineering, Mathematical Model and Calculation, 
						Artificial Intelligence. All topics were interesting and 
						reflect the current focus of research and development in 
						the field.

ICCMS 2020
JUNE 22-24, 2020 |
Virtual Conference (Brisbane, Australia)
ICCMS 2020 was originally scheduled to be held at 
						Central Queensland University, Brisbane, Australia 
						during February 26-28, 2020. Due to the COVID-19 
						pandemic and pervasive travel restrictions worldwide, 
						the Conferences had to be postponed to June 22-24 and 
						held online. Scientists, scholars, engineers and 
						research students from over 20 countries and districts 
						gathered via Zoom and shared their research works.
The conference lasts for three days. Four renowned 
						keynote speakers presented speeches in industry modeling 
						and simulation respectively. These keynote speeches were 
						inspirational to and well received by the audiences. 
						Followed these keynote speeches, ten parallel sessions 
						were successfully held in two days with collaborative 
						effort from individual presenters and the session 
						chairs.

Presenter Instruction
Instructions for Oral Presentations
âª Each speaker is allocated 15 minutes for your talk, 
						including the time for Q&A and for the changeover 
						between speakers, so please aim for no more than 12 
						minutes for the presentation itself. The slides and the 
						presentations must be in English.
âª In each oral presentation room, Laptop (with MS-Office 
						& Adobe Reader), projector & screen and laser pointer 
						will be available. You need to meet the chair of your 
						session 10 minutes before session start and test your 
						slides.
Instructions for Poster 
						Presentations
1. Poster Dimensions
Poster Size: A1 Size 23.4 inches (59.4 cm) wide x 33.1 
						inches (84.1 cm) high.
Please note that printing out your submitted full paper 
						in A4 size format is NOT acceptable.
2. Poster Content
Title: The conference short name, paper ID, and paper 
						title should appear at the top.
Text: Text should be readable from five feet away. Keep 
						the text brief. Try to use text to introduce the study, 
						explain visuals and direct viewers' attention to 
						significant data trends and relationships portrayed in 
						the visuals, state and explain the interpretations that 
						follow from the data.
Figures/ Tables: Each figure should have a brief title. 
						Try to use color figures rather than only black and 
						white text to make your poster attractive and highlight 
						the important technical content of your paper. Make sure 
						that the text and the visuals are integrated.
3. Poster Display
Author should post up your poster on the poster panel in 
						the morning of the day of your poster session.
Please note that your poster should be prepared well in 
						advance.
Please remove your poster before end of the day, or it 
						will be discarded.

Call for Papers
The 
									18th International Conference on Computer Modeling and Simulation (ICCMS 
									2026) is the premier forum for the presentation of 
						technological advances and research results in the 
						fields of Computer Modeling and Simulation. ICCMS 2026 
						will bring together leading engineers and scientists in 
									the field of Computer Modeling and Simulation from around the world.
Topics of interest for 
						submission include, but are not limited to:
Track 1: Fundamental Theories andMethodologies- Mathematical modeling and simulation techniques- Computational algorithms for simulation- Discrete-event simulation- Continuous and hybrid simulation models- Uncertainty quantification in simulationsTrack 2: Artificial Intelligence andMachine 
							Learning in SimulationTrack Chairs:Prof. Fei Guo, Chongqing 
							University of Posts and Telecommunications, ChinaAssoc. Prof. Zhongyong Zhao, Southwest University, 
							China- AI-driven modeling and simulation- Deep learning for predictive simulations- Reinforcement learning in dynamic systems- Neural networks for simulation optimization- Explainable AI in computational modelsTrack 3: High-Performance andLarge-Scale 
							Simulations- Parallel and distributed simulation- Cloud/edge computing for scalable simulations- GPU-accelerated modeling techniques- Big data analytics in simulation- Cyberinfrastructure for large-scale simulations | Track 4: Applications in 
							Engineering and Science- Computational fluid dynamics (CFD)- Structural and mechanical simulations- Climate and environmental modeling- Biomedical and healthcare simulations- Energy systems and smart grid simulationsTrack 5: Social, Economic, and Urban Systems 
							Simulation- Agent-based modeling for social systems- Economic forecasting and policy simulations- Urban planning and smart city simulations- Traffic and transportation modeling- Disaster response and crisis management 
							simulationsTrack 6: Emerging Technologies and Industry 4.0- Digital twins and virtual prototyping- IoT-enabled simulation systems- Robotics and autonomous systems simulation- Blockchain in simulation security

Reviewer Application
ICCMS conference 
						submissions are subject to a double-blind peer review 
						process by reviewers. Reviewers are members the 
						conference's Technical Committee of experts in fields 
						specifically matching the conference topics.
ICCMS 2026 welcomes competent scholars to join us as 
						reviewers. There are great benefits to becoming a 
						reviewer; including staying up-to-date with the latest 
						literature, advancing your career and establishing your 
						expertise and reputation in the field. ICCMS follows a 
						strict set of guidelines while accepting the applicant 
						as a reviewer. The minimum set of qualifications for 
						being a reviewer are listed below:
* Should be a holder of PhD degree from a recognized 
						university.
* Should be an author with a minimum of a 2 publications 
						in reputed journals or conferences.
If you would like to volunteer to become a reviewer for 
						ICCMS 2026 we recommend that you read more about the 
						peer review process. Peer review may seem like a 
						thankless task, but without it research would be 
						unreliable; ICCMS 2026 offer several incentives to 
						reward our reviewers.
To sign up as reviewer, please send your latest CV to 
						E-mail:
iccms_contact@163.com

Peer Review
The works should be 
						original, unpublished, and not in consideration for 
						publication elsewhere at the time of submission to the 
						18th International Conference on Computer Modeling and 
						Simulation (ICCMS 2026) and during the review process.
ICCMS 2026 provides double blind peer review for all the 
						material submitted. Some submissions are rejected 
						without being sent out for external peer review on the 
						grounds of priority, insufficient originality and 
						scientific flaws. A decision on such papers is taken 
						quickly, usually within 2-3 weeks.
The remaining manuscripts should be evaluated by two 
						reviewers with expertise in the relevant subject area 
						and by the anonymous program chair of the program 
						committee, usually within 2 weeks. Authors are welcome 
						to suggest suitable independent reviewers.
Once the paper draft was reviewed and corrected draft 
						(if it necessary) after comments from reviewers were 
						received final decision concerning the publishing is 
						made by the program committee usually within one week.
The program committee is the competent authority in 
						taking decision(s) on selection or rejection of the 
						manuscripts of all the research/review/similar articles 
						submitted to ICCMS 2026. The decision taken by program 
						committee is final in all respects and no further 
						comments/communications from authors in this regard is 
						expected.

=== PAGE: https://iccms.org/visit.html ===
Link: Discover Hangzhou
Title: 18th ICCMS 2026-Computer Modeling and Simulation 第十八届计算机建模与仿真国际会议
18th ICCMS 2026-Computer Modeling and Simulation 第十八届计算机建模与仿真国际会议
iccms_contact@163.com
+86-18302820449
Discover Hangzhou
Hangzhou, 
									the
																		capital of Zhejiang Province and Song 
									Dynasty capital, is known as 'Heaven on 
									Earth', with its stunning natural beauty and 
									rich cultural heritage. It was described by 
									Marco Polo as "the finest and most splendid 
									city in the world".
									Hangzhou is the political, economic, 
									cultural, educational, transportation, and 
									financial center of Zhejiang Province. The 
									2022 Asian Games was held in Hangzhou.
Where Hangzhou Is Located
Hangzhou is on the east coast of China, 
									south of the Yangtze River Delta and about 
									170 kilometers (105 miles) south of 
									Shanghai. By bullet train, Hangzhou is about 
									1 hour from Shanghai and 1.5 hours from 
									Suzhou.
The Features 
									of Hangzhou
▪
Hangzhou is
one of the Seven Ancient Capital Cities
, 
									with a history of about 2,500 years. Take a 
									cruise to explore the World Heritage Site - 
									the Grand Canal.
▪
Hangzhou boasts
enchanting natural scenery
.
West Lake
, the symbol of Hangzhou, is 
									considered to be one of the most beautiful 
									sights in China.
▪
Hangzhou offers
a variety of traditional cultures
, 
									such as tea, traditional Chinese medicine, 
									and Chinese silk.
▪
Hangzhou has a
highly developed transportation network
. 
									It is almost the only city in China 
									following 'pedestrian first' laws.
Hangzhou Is a 
									Leisurely City
Hangzhou is a special city. It is developing 
									at a high speed, while the lifestyle in 
									Hangzhou is leisurely. Compared to Shanghai, 
									the pace of life there is slow.
When traveling in Hangzhou, you can have a 
									stroll by West Lake, take a boat cruise, 
									feed the goldfish in the lake, and enjoy the 
									beautiful scenery. Then have a delicious 
									afternoon tea by the lake and chat with your 
									guide, learning about the story and history 
									of Hangzhou.
If you want to feel the classical charm of 
									Hangzhou, you can pay a visit to some water 
									towns. There are old buildings, boats, 
									rivers, ancient bridges, and wells. 
									Everything there is peaceful and beautiful.
If you want to know more about the tea 
									culture of Hangzhou, you can go to Meijiawu 
									Tea Plantation for an idyllic tour. Pick tea 
									leaves with a local tea farmer, watch a 
									Chinese tea ceremony, and learn about 
									Chinese tea culture.
5 Highlights 
									of Hangzhou
Hangzhou is a 
									perfect combination of history and 
									modernity, nature and culture. When you 
									visit Hangzhou, don't miss the following 
									highlights.
1. West Lake - the Legend of Hangzhou
West Lake is a symbol of Hangzhou. It is 
									famous for its stunning scenery and various 
									historical sites. It was selected as one of 
									the "12 superb sunset spots around the 
									world" by CNN.
West Lake
is gorgeous all year round. The spring 
									flowers, the summer lotuses, the autumn 
									moon, and the winter snow all make it very 
									charming.
2. Water Towns 
									Near Hangzhou
Wuzhen and Nanxun are the two most popular 
									water towns near Hangzhou.
Wuzhen
is one of the top six water towns in 
									China. Taking a gondola along the ancient 
									canal should not be missed and the nighttime 
									scenery in Wuzhen is impressive.
Nanxun
is not as famous as Wuzhen. It is thanks to 
									this that Nanxun is the most original and 
									picturesque town, filled with a tranquil 
									ambiance.
3. The Grand 
									Canal - World Heritage Site
The Grand Canal was dug out in 486 BC and 
									has a history of more than 2,500 years. It 
									runs from Beijing in the north to Hangzhou 
									in the south, stretching for 1,747 
									kilometers.
It played a great role in the economic and 
									cultural development, and with exchanges 
									between the north and south of China. It was 
									a great project created by the ancient 
									Chinese and is now one of the symbols of 
									Chinese culture.
4. Dragon Well Tea
Hangzhou's unique Dragon Well tea (Longjing 
									Tea) plays an important role among the 
									varieties of Chinese green tea. It is 
									produced mostly by hand, and is honored as 
									China's Famous Tea due to its high quality.
5. Hangzhou Silk
Hangzhou is regarded as the cradle of the 
									Chinese silk culture and production. With a 
									history of over 2,500 years, Hangzhou silk 
									is renowned for its gentle texture, gorgeous 
									colors, and wide varieties.
Convenient Transportation Network
Hangzhou is easily accessible by high-speed 
									trains and flights from main cities, such as 
									Beijing, Shanghai, Suzhou, Xi'an, Chengdu, 
									and Hong Kong.
From Hangzhou, the direct bus is the most 
									convenient way to get to nearby water towns. 
									A private tour with a private vehicle is the 
									best hassle-free option to avoid the traffic 
									congestion and help you maximize your time.
See more on
Hangzhou transportation
.
Emergency Phone 
									Numbers
▪ 110 for police
▪ 112 for traffic police (emergency only)
▪ 119 for fire services
▪ 120 for medical assistance or an ambulance
If you encounter a really complex situation, 
									call 110 first.

Visa Information
Chinese 
						Visa
Conference participants may need a Chinese visa to enter 
						China. Specifically, those who are not Chinese citizens, 
						except passport holders from Singapore, Brunei, and 
						Japan, need a Chinese visa. Participants can apply for a 
						visa at the Chinese embassy or consulate in the region 
						in which they live. To avoid uncertainty, participants 
						will be advised to apply for a visa as early as 
						possible. It is recommended that they apply for a 
						Chinese visa at least 1 month in advance.
For most attendees, it is easier to apply for a Tourist 
						("L") visa; an invitation letter is NOT required for 
						obtaining a Tourist visa.
Necessary materials to apply 
						for Chinese Visa
Â»A passport which is valid for at least six months 
						following the date of submission, with an entire blank 
						page available for the visa;
Â»One Visa Application Form of the People's Republic of 
						China, which is filled clearly with true, authentic and 
						complete information and signed by the applicant;
Â»One recent, 2 inch full-faced and bareheaded passport 
						photo (the daily life photo, photo copy or digital photo 
						printed on the ordinary paper shall not be accepted);
Â»Other related application materials according to 
						different purposes for your trip to China;
Â»Other materials related to the visa application, deemed 
						as necessary by the visa officer.
* For more information, please contact the local Chinese 
						Embassy in your country.
Invitation Letters
1. The invitation letter will be issued after the 
						successful registration.
2. Your name must be listed exactly as it appears on 
						your passport. Any differences between the name on your 
						passport and the name on your invitation letter or other 
						documentation could lead to a delay and/or denial of 
						your visa.
3. Please note ICCMS Conferences and the organizing 
						committee of the conference are not authorized to assist 
						with the VISA process beyond providing the Invitation 
						Letter issued by ICCMS Conferences and conference 
						Committee Board. Should your application be denied, 
						ICCMS Conferences and the organizing committee of the 
						conference cannot change the decision of the Ministry of 
						Foreign Affairs, nor will ICCMS Conferences and the 
						organizing committee of the conference engage in 
						discussion or correspondence with the MOFA or the 
						Embassy on behalf of the applicant. The registration fee 
						cannot be refunded when the VISA application of 
						individual being denied.
4. It takes around one month for the Embassy to process 
						the VISA application, please register as early as you 
						can to make sure you have enough time.

.

Prof. Seifedine Kadry (IET Fellow)
Lebanese American University, USA
Seifedine Kadry is a 
									professor of Data Science at Lebanese 
									American university, USA. He holds a HdR 
									(Habilitation Degree) in Engineering 
									Sciences from Rouen University, France 
									(2017) and a Ph.D. in Engineering Sciences 
									from Blaise-Pascal University, France 
									(2007). He also earned an M.S. in Applied 
									Mathematics and Computer Science from EPFL 
									Lausanne, Switzerland (2002) and a B.S. in 
									Computer Science and Applied Mathematics 
									from the Lebanese University (1999). 
									Previously, he was a professor at the 
									Department of Mathematics and Computer 
									Science at Beirut Arab University and the 
									founder and director of the Data Science 
									Center there. He also held academic 
									positions at the American University of the 
									Middle East, Kuwait, and Lebanese 
									University, Lebanon.

ICCMS 2025
June 13-15, 2025 |
Zhuhai, China
The 17th International Conference on Computer Modeling 
						and Simulation (ICCMS 2025) was held in Zhuhai, China 
						during June 13-15, 2025 successfully. ICCMS 2025 is 
						co-organized by Intelligent IoT System Modeling and 
						Simulation Committee of China Simulation Federation, 
						China and Beihang University, China, co-hosted by Jinan 
						University (Zhuhai Campus), China; The Hong Kong 
						Polytechnic University, Hong Kong, China and Hangzhou 
						International Innovation Institute of Beihang 
						University, China. The conference featured 14 thematic 
						sub-forums covering hot topics such as intelligent 
						modeling and simulation, modeling and optimization 
						calculation in human-computer collaboration and 
						interactive design, Ssructural design, functional 
						simulation and simulation analysis in mechanical and 
						manufacturing systems.

Virtual Conference (Dalian, China)
The 16th International Conference on Computer Modeling and Simulation 
						(ICCMS 2024) was held virtually during 
						June 21-23, 2024. ICCMS 
						2024 was organized by Dalian University of Technology, 
						China, co-organized by Chongqing University of Posts and 
						Telecommunications, China and Heilongjiang Province Key 
						Laboratory of Laser Spectroscopy Technology and 
						Application, China.
Three keynote speakers and one invited speaker shared 
						with us their research works. They are Prof. Alejandro F 
						Frangi (IEEE Fellow & SPIE Fellow), The University of 
						Manchester, UK; Prof. Xiao Song, Beihang University, 
						China; Prof. Xiangjian He, University of Nottingham 
						Ningbo China, China.
ICCMS 2024 provided a professional exposure and exchange 
						platform for academicians, researchers, scientists, 
						engineers and technologists internationally, who focus 
						on the field of Computer Modeling and Simulation, to 
						share their researches and collaborate through 
						conference discussions.

						scheduled to be held in Melbourne, Australia, but due to 
						the outbreak of the COVID pandemic, the conference was 
						switched to the virtual conference form. ICCMS 2021 was 
						co-organized by Swinburne University of Technology, 
						Australia and University of Wollongong, Australia, and 
						technically supported by Chongqing University of Post 
						and Telecommunications, China.
Three renowned keynote speakers shared with us their 
						research works. They are Prof. Ming C. Lin (ACM Fellow, 
						IEEE Fellow) from University of Maryland, College Park, 
						USA; Prof. Makoto Iwasaki (IEEE Fellow) from Nagoya 
						Institute of Technology, Japan; Prof. Marimuthu 
						Palaniswami (IEEE Fellow) from The University of 
						Melbourne, Australia. Besides, there were three invited 
						speakers shared their latest research results with us.
Authors from Japan, Australia, Austria, The Netherlands, 
						China, etc. were divided into 7 parallel sessions. Their 
						presentations covered topics such as Computer and 
						Information Technology, Mechanical and Control 
						Engineering, Mathematical Model and Calculation, 
						Artificial Intelligence. All topics were interesting and 
						reflect the current focus of research and development in 
						the field.

Call for Papers
The 
									18th International Conference on Computer Modeling and Simulation (ICCMS 
									2026) is the premier forum for the presentation of 
						technological advances and research results in the 
						fields of Computer Modeling and Simulation. ICCMS 2026 
						will bring together leading engineers and scientists in 
									the field of Computer Modeling and Simulation from around the world.
Topics of interest for 
						submission include, but are not limited to:
Track 1: Fundamental Theories andMethodologies- Mathematical modeling and simulation techniques- Computational algorithms for simulation- Discrete-event simulation- Continuous and hybrid simulation models- Uncertainty quantification in simulationsTrack 2: Artificial Intelligence andMachine 
							Learning in SimulationTrack Chairs:Prof. Fei Guo, Chongqing 
							University of Posts and Telecommunications, ChinaAssoc. Prof. Zhongyong Zhao, Southwest University, 
							China- AI-driven modeling and simulation- Deep learning for predictive simulations- Reinforcement learning in dynamic systems- Neural networks for simulation optimization- Explainable AI in computational modelsTrack 3: High-Performance andLarge-Scale 
							Simulations- Parallel and distributed simulation- Cloud/edge computing for scalable simulations- GPU-accelerated modeling techniques- Big data analytics in simulation- Cyberinfrastructure for large-scale simulations | Track 4: Applications in 
							Engineering and Science- Computational fluid dynamics (CFD)- Structural and mechanical simulations- Climate and environmental modeling- Biomedical and healthcare simulations- Energy systems and smart grid simulationsTrack 5: Social, Economic, and Urban Systems 
							Simulation- Agent-based modeling for social systems- Economic forecasting and policy simulations- Urban planning and smart city simulations- Traffic and transportation modeling- Disaster response and crisis management 
							simulationsTrack 6: Emerging Technologies and Industry 4.0- Digital twins and virtual prototyping- IoT-enabled simulation systems- Robotics and autonomous systems simulation- Blockchain in simulation security

Reviewer Application
ICCMS conference 
						submissions are subject to a double-blind peer review 
						process by reviewers. Reviewers are members the 
						conference's Technical Committee of experts in fields 
						specifically matching the conference topics.
ICCMS 2026 welcomes competent scholars to join us as 
						reviewers. There are great benefits to becoming a 
						reviewer; including staying up-to-date with the latest 
						literature, advancing your career and establishing your 
						expertise and reputation in the field. ICCMS follows a 
						strict set of guidelines while accepting the applicant 
						as a reviewer. The minimum set of qualifications for 
						being a reviewer are listed below:
* Should be a holder of PhD degree from a recognized 
						university.
* Should be an author with a minimum of a 2 publications 
						in reputed journals or conferences.
If you would like to volunteer to become a reviewer for 
						ICCMS 2026 we recommend that you read more about the 
						peer review process. Peer review may seem like a 
						thankless task, but without it research would be 
						unreliable; ICCMS 2026 offer several incentives to 
						reward our reviewers.
To sign up as reviewer, please send your latest CV to 
						E-mail:
iccms_contact@163.com

Peer Review
The works should be 
						original, unpublished, and not in consideration for 
						publication elsewhere at the time of submission to the 
						18th International Conference on Computer Modeling and 
						Simulation (ICCMS 2026) and during the review process.
ICCMS 2026 provides double blind peer review for all the 
						material submitted. Some submissions are rejected 
						without being sent out for external peer review on the 
						grounds of priority, insufficient originality and 
						scientific flaws. A decision on such papers is taken 
						quickly, usually within 2-3 weeks.
The remaining manuscripts should be evaluated by two 
						reviewers with expertise in the relevant subject area 
						and by the anonymous program chair of the program 
						committee, usually within 2 weeks. Authors are welcome 
						to suggest suitable independent reviewers.
Once the paper draft was reviewed and corrected draft 
						(if it necessary) after comments from reviewers were 
						received final decision concerning the publishing is 
						made by the program committee usually within one week.
The program committee is the competent authority in 
						taking decision(s) on selection or rejection of the 
						manuscripts of all the research/review/similar articles 
						submitted to ICCMS 2026. The decision taken by program 
						committee is final in all respects and no further 
						comments/communications from authors in this regard is 
						expected.

Presenter Instruction
Instructions for Oral Presentations
âª Each speaker is allocated 15 minutes for your talk, 
						including the time for Q&A and for the changeover 
						between speakers, so please aim for no more than 12 
						minutes for the presentation itself. The slides and the 
						presentations must be in English.
âª In each oral presentation room, Laptop (with MS-Office 
						& Adobe Reader), projector & screen and laser pointer 
						will be available. You need to meet the chair of your 
						session 10 minutes before session start and test your 
						slides.
Instructions for Poster 
						Presentations
1. Poster Dimensions
Poster Size: A1 Size 23.4 inches (59.4 cm) wide x 33.1 
						inches (84.1 cm) high.
Please note that printing out your submitted full paper 
						in A4 size format is NOT acceptable.
2. Poster Content
Title: The conference short name, paper ID, and paper 
						title should appear at the top.
Text: Text should be readable from five feet away. Keep 
						the text brief. Try to use text to introduce the study, 
						explain visuals and direct viewers' attention to 
						significant data trends and relationships portrayed in 
						the visuals, state and explain the interpretations that 
						follow from the data.
Figures/ Tables: Each figure should have a brief title. 
						Try to use color figures rather than only black and 
						white text to make your poster attractive and highlight 
						the important technical content of your paper. Make sure 
						that the text and the visuals are integrated.
3. Poster Display
Author should post up your poster on the poster panel in 
						the morning of the day of your poster session.
Please note that your poster should be prepared well in 
						advance.
Please remove your poster before end of the day, or it 
						will be discarded.

=== PAGE: https://iccms.org/visit.html ===
Link: Discover Hangzhou
Title: 18th ICCMS 2026-Computer Modeling and Simulation 第十八届计算机建模与仿真国际会议
18th ICCMS 2026-Computer Modeling and Simulation 第十八届计算机建模与仿真国际会议
iccms_contact@163.com
+86-18302820449
Discover Hangzhou
Hangzhou, 
									the
																		capital of Zhejiang Province and Song 
									Dynasty capital, is known as 'Heaven on 
									Earth', with its stunning natural beauty and 
									rich cultural heritage. It was described by 
									Marco Polo as "the finest and most splendid 
									city in the world".
									Hangzhou is the political, economic, 
									cultural, educational, transportation, and 
									financial center of Zhejiang Province. The 
									2022 Asian Games was held in Hangzhou.
Where Hangzhou Is Located
Hangzhou is on the east coast of China, 
									south of the Yangtze River Delta and about 
									170 kilometers (105 miles) south of 
									Shanghai. By bullet train, Hangzhou is about 
									1 hour from Shanghai and 1.5 hours from 
									Suzhou.
The Features 
									of Hangzhou
▪
Hangzhou is
one of the Seven Ancient Capital Cities
, 
									with a history of about 2,500 years. Take a 
									cruise to explore the World Heritage Site - 
									the Grand Canal.
▪
Hangzhou boasts
enchanting natural scenery
.
West Lake
, the symbol of Hangzhou, is 
									considered to be one of the most beautiful 
									sights in China.
▪
Hangzhou offers
a variety of traditional cultures
, 
									such as tea, traditional Chinese medicine, 
									and Chinese silk.
▪
Hangzhou has a
highly developed transportation network
. 
									It is almost the only city in China 
									following 'pedestrian first' laws.
Hangzhou Is a 
									Leisurely City
Hangzhou is a special city. It is developing 
									at a high speed, while the lifestyle in 
									Hangzhou is leisurely. Compared to Shanghai, 
									the pace of life there is slow.
When traveling in Hangzhou, you can have a 
									stroll by West Lake, take a boat cruise, 
									feed the goldfish in the lake, and enjoy the 
									beautiful scenery. Then have a delicious 
									afternoon tea by the lake and chat with your 
									guide, learning about the story and history 
									of Hangzhou.
If you want to feel the classical charm of 
									Hangzhou, you can pay a visit to some water 
									towns. There are old buildings, boats, 
									rivers, ancient bridges, and wells. 
									Everything there is peaceful and beautiful.
If you want to know more about the tea 
									culture of Hangzhou, you can go to Meijiawu 
									Tea Plantation for an idyllic tour. Pick tea 
									leaves with a local tea farmer, watch a 
									Chinese tea ceremony, and learn about 
									Chinese tea culture.
5 Highlights 
									of Hangzhou
Hangzhou is a 
									perfect combination of history and 
									modernity, nature and culture. When you 
									visit Hangzhou, don't miss the following 
									highlights.
1. West Lake - the Legend of Hangzhou
West Lake is a symbol of Hangzhou. It is 
									famous for its stunning scenery and various 
									historical sites. It was selected as one of 
									the "12 superb sunset spots around the 
									world" by CNN.
West Lake
is gorgeous all year round. The spring 
									flowers, the summer lotuses, the autumn 
									moon, and the winter snow all make it very 
									charming.
2. Water Towns 
									Near Hangzhou
Wuzhen and Nanxun are the two most popular 
									water towns near Hangzhou.
Wuzhen
is one of the top six water towns in 
									China. Taking a gondola along the ancient 
									canal should not be missed and the nighttime 
									scenery in Wuzhen is impressive.
Nanxun
is not as famous as Wuzhen. It is thanks to 
									this that Nanxun is the most original and 
									picturesque town, filled with a tranquil 
									ambiance.
3. The Grand 
									Canal - World Heritage Site
The Grand Canal was dug out in 486 BC and 
									has a history of more than 2,500 years. It 
									runs from Beijing in the north to Hangzhou 
									in the south, stretching for 1,747 
									kilometers.
It played a great role in the economic and 
									cultural development, and with exchanges 
									between the north and south of China. It was 
									a great project created by the ancient 
									Chinese and is now one of the symbols of 
									Chinese culture.
4. Dragon Well Tea
Hangzhou's unique Dragon Well tea (Longjing 
									Tea) plays an important role among the 
									varieties of Chinese green tea. It is 
									produced mostly by hand, and is honored as 
									China's Famous Tea due to its high quality.
5. Hangzhou Silk
Hangzhou is regarded as the cradle of the 
									Chinese silk culture and production. With a 
									history of over 2,500 years, Hangzhou silk 
									is renowned for its gentle texture, gorgeous 
									colors, and wide varieties.
Convenient Transportation Network
Hangzhou is easily accessible by high-speed 
									trains and flights from main cities, such as 
									Beijing, Shanghai, Suzhou, Xi'an, Chengdu, 
									and Hong Kong.
From Hangzhou, the direct bus is the most 
									convenient way to get to nearby water towns. 
									A private tour with a private vehicle is the 
									best hassle-free option to avoid the traffic 
									congestion and help you maximize your time.
See more on
Hangzhou transportation
.
Emergency Phone 
									Numbers
▪ 110 for police
▪ 112 for traffic police (emergency only)
▪ 119 for fire services
▪ 120 for medical assistance or an ambulance
If you encounter a really complex situation, 
									call 110 first.

Visa Information
Chinese 
						Visa
Conference participants may need a Chinese visa to enter 
						China. Specifically, those who are not Chinese citizens, 
						except passport holders from Singapore, Brunei, and 
						Japan, need a Chinese visa. Participants can apply for a 
						visa at the Chinese embassy or consulate in the region 
						in which they live. To avoid uncertainty, participants 
						will be advised to apply for a visa as early as 
						possible. It is recommended that they apply for a 
						Chinese visa at least 1 month in advance.
For most attendees, it is easier to apply for a Tourist 
						("L") visa; an invitation letter is NOT required for 
						obtaining a Tourist visa.
Necessary materials to apply 
						for Chinese Visa
Â»A passport which is valid for at least six months 
						following the date of submission, with an entire blank 
						page available for the visa;
Â»One Visa Application Form of the People's Republic of 
						China, which is filled clearly with true, authentic and 
						complete information and signed by the applicant;
Â»One recent, 2 inch full-faced and bareheaded passport 
						photo (the daily life photo, photo copy or digital photo 
						printed on the ordinary paper shall not be accepted);
Â»Other related application materials according to 
						different purposes for your trip to China;
Â»Other materials related to the visa application, deemed 
						as necessary by the visa officer.
* For more information, please contact the local Chinese 
						Embassy in your country.
Invitation Letters
1. The invitation letter will be issued after the 
						successful registration.
2. Your name must be listed exactly as it appears on 
						your passport. Any differences between the name on your 
						passport and the name on your invitation letter or other 
						documentation could lead to a delay and/or denial of 
						your visa.
3. Please note ICCMS Conferences and the organizing 
						committee of the conference are not authorized to assist 
						with the VISA process beyond providing the Invitation 
						Letter issued by ICCMS Conferences and conference 
						Committee Board. Should your application be denied, 
						ICCMS Conferences and the organizing committee of the 
						conference cannot change the decision of the Ministry of 
						Foreign Affairs, nor will ICCMS Conferences and the 
						organizing committee of the conference engage in 
						discussion or correspondence with the MOFA or the 
						Embassy on behalf of the applicant. The registration fee 
						cannot be refunded when the VISA application of 
						individual being denied.
4. It takes around one month for the Embassy to process 
						the VISA application, please register as early as you 
						can to make sure you have enough time.

no less than 5

, including all figures, tables, and

ICCMS 2025
ICCMS 2025
June 13-15, 2025 |
Zhuhai, China
The 17th International Conference on Computer Modeling 
						and Simulation (ICCMS 2025) was held in Zhuhai, China 
						during June 13-15, 2025 successfully. ICCMS 2025 is 
						co-organized by Intelligent IoT System Modeling and 
						Simulation Committee of China Simulation Federation, 
						China and Beihang University, China, co-hosted by Jinan 
						University (Zhuhai Campus), China; The Hong Kong 
						Polytechnic University, Hong Kong, China and Hangzhou 
						International Innovation Institute of Beihang 
						University, China. The conference featured 14 thematic 
						sub-forums covering hot topics such as intelligent 
						modeling and simulation, modeling and optimization 
						calculation in human-computer collaboration and 
						interactive design, Ssructural design, functional 
						simulation and simulation analysis in mechanical and 
						manufacturing systems.

						2022 was sponsored by Chongqing University of Posts and 
						Telecommunications, China; co-sponsored by University of 
						Wollongong, Australia and assisted by Chongqing 
						Association for Young Science and Technology leaders.
Four keynote speakers shared with us their research 
						works. They are Prof. Ming C. Lin (ACM Fellow, IEEE 
						Fellow) from University of Maryland, College Park, USA; 
						Prof. Alejandro F Frangi (IEEE Fellow, SPIE Fellow) from 
						University of Leeds, UK; Prof. Karen Panetta (IEEE 
						Fellow) from Tufts University, USA; Prof. Lin Zhang (SCS 
						Fellow) from Beihang University, China. In addition, 
						four invited speakers had a deep discussion on their 
						latest researches with us. This year we totally have 51 
						author presenters from all over the world, such as 
						Australia, China, Russia, Thailand, The Netherlands, 
						USA, etc.
ICCMS 2022 provided a professional exposure and exchange 
						platform for academicians, researchers, scientists, 
						engineers and technologists internationally, who focus 
						on the field of Computer Modeling and Simulation, to 
						share their researches and collaborate through 
						conference discussions.
The official website of Chongqing University of Posts and Telecommunications, 
China posts ICCMS 2022 conference information.

ICCMS 2021
ICCMS 2021
JUNE 25-27, 2021 |
Virtual Conference (Melbourne, Australia)
The 13th International Conference on Computer Modeling 
						and Simulation (ICCMS 2021) has been held online during 
						June 25-27, 2021. The conference had initially been 
						scheduled to be held in Melbourne, Australia, but due to 
						the outbreak of the COVID pandemic, the conference was 
						switched to the virtual conference form. ICCMS 2021 was 
						co-organized by Swinburne University of Technology, 
						Australia and University of Wollongong, Australia, and 
						technically supported by Chongqing University of Post 
						and Telecommunications, China.
Three renowned keynote speakers shared with us their 
						research works. They are Prof. Ming C. Lin (ACM Fellow, 
						IEEE Fellow) from University of Maryland, College Park, 
						USA; Prof. Makoto Iwasaki (IEEE Fellow) from Nagoya 
						Institute of Technology, Japan; Prof. Marimuthu 
						Palaniswami (IEEE Fellow) from The University of 
						Melbourne, Australia. Besides, there were three invited 
						speakers shared their latest research results with us.
Authors from Japan, Australia, Austria, The Netherlands, 
						China, etc. were divided into 7 parallel sessions. Their 
						presentations covered topics such as Computer and 
						Information Technology, Mechanical and Control 
						Engineering, Mathematical Model and Calculation, 
						Artificial Intelligence. All topics were interesting and 
						reflect the current focus of research and development in 
						the field.

ICCMS 2020
JUNE 22-24, 2020 |
Virtual Conference (Brisbane, Australia)
ICCMS 2020 was originally scheduled to be held at 
						Central Queensland University, Brisbane, Australia 
						during February 26-28, 2020. Due to the COVID-19 
						pandemic and pervasive travel restrictions worldwide, 
						the Conferences had to be postponed to June 22-24 and 
						held online. Scientists, scholars, engineers and 
						research students from over 20 countries and districts 
						gathered via Zoom and shared their research works.
The conference lasts for three days. Four renowned 
						keynote speakers presented speeches in industry modeling 
						and simulation respectively. These keynote speeches were 
						inspirational to and well received by the audiences. 
						Followed these keynote speeches, ten parallel sessions 
						were successfully held in two days with collaborative 
						effort from individual presenters and the session 
						chairs.

Call for Papers
The 
									18th International Conference on Computer Modeling and Simulation (ICCMS 
									2026) is the premier forum for the presentation of 
						technological advances and research results in the 
						fields of Computer Modeling and Simulation. ICCMS 2026 
						will bring together leading engineers and scientists in 
									the field of Computer Modeling and Simulation from around the world.
Topics of interest for 
						submission include, but are not limited to:
Track 1: Fundamental Theories andMethodologies- Mathematical modeling and simulation techniques- Computational algorithms for simulation- Discrete-event simulation- Continuous and hybrid simulation models- Uncertainty quantification in simulationsTrack 2: Artificial Intelligence andMachine

							China- AI-driven modeling and simulation- Deep learning for predictive simulations- Reinforcement learning in dynamic systems- Neural networks for simulation optimization- Explainable AI in computational modelsTrack 3: High-Performance andLarge-Scale

							Engineering and Science- Computational fluid dynamics (CFD)- Structural and mechanical simulations- Climate and environmental modeling- Biomedical and healthcare simulations- Energy systems and smart grid simulationsTrack 5: Social, Economic, and Urban Systems

Reviewer Application
ICCMS conference 
						submissions are subject to a double-blind peer review 
						process by reviewers. Reviewers are members the 
						conference's Technical Committee of experts in fields 
						specifically matching the conference topics.
ICCMS 2026 welcomes competent scholars to join us as 
						reviewers. There are great benefits to becoming a 
						reviewer; including staying up-to-date with the latest 
						literature, advancing your career and establishing your 
						expertise and reputation in the field. ICCMS follows a 
						strict set of guidelines while accepting the applicant 
						as a reviewer. The minimum set of qualifications for 
						being a reviewer are listed below:
* Should be a holder of PhD degree from a recognized 
						university.
* Should be an author with a minimum of a 2 publications 
						in reputed journals or conferences.
If you would like to volunteer to become a reviewer for 
						ICCMS 2026 we recommend that you read more about the 
						peer review process. Peer review may seem like a 
						thankless task, but without it research would be 
						unreliable; ICCMS 2026 offer several incentives to 
						reward our reviewers.
To sign up as reviewer, please send your latest CV to 
						E-mail:
iccms_contact@163.com

Peer Review
The works should be 
						original, unpublished, and not in consideration for 
						publication elsewhere at the time of submission to the 
						18th International Conference on Computer Modeling and 
						Simulation (ICCMS 2026) and during the review process.
ICCMS 2026 provides double blind peer review for all the 
						material submitted. Some submissions are rejected 
						without being sent out for external peer review on the 
						grounds of priority, insufficient originality and 
						scientific flaws. A decision on such papers is taken 
						quickly, usually within 2-3 weeks.
The remaining manuscripts should be evaluated by two 
						reviewers with expertise in the relevant subject area 
						and by the anonymous program chair of the program 
						committee, usually within 2 weeks. Authors are welcome 
						to suggest suitable independent reviewers.
Once the paper draft was reviewed and corrected draft 
						(if it necessary) after comments from reviewers were 
						received final decision concerning the publishing is 
						made by the program committee usually within one week.
The program committee is the competent authority in 
						taking decision(s) on selection or rejection of the 
						manuscripts of all the research/review/similar articles 
						submitted to ICCMS 2026. The decision taken by program 
						committee is final in all respects and no further 
						comments/communications from authors in this regard is 
						expected.

Visa Information
Chinese 
						Visa
Conference participants may need a Chinese visa to enter 
						China. Specifically, those who are not Chinese citizens, 
						except passport holders from Singapore, Brunei, and 
						Japan, need a Chinese visa. Participants can apply for a 
						visa at the Chinese embassy or consulate in the region 
						in which they live. To avoid uncertainty, participants 
						will be advised to apply for a visa as early as 
						possible. It is recommended that they apply for a 
						Chinese visa at least 1 month in advance.
For most attendees, it is easier to apply for a Tourist 
						("L") visa; an invitation letter is NOT required for 
						obtaining a Tourist visa.
Necessary materials to apply 
						for Chinese Visa
Â»A passport which is valid for at least six months 
						following the date of submission, with an entire blank 
						page available for the visa;
Â»One Visa Application Form of the People's Republic of 
						China, which is filled clearly with true, authentic and 
						complete information and signed by the applicant;
Â»One recent, 2 inch full-faced and bareheaded passport 
						photo (the daily life photo, photo copy or digital photo 
						printed on the ordinary paper shall not be accepted);
Â»Other related application materials according to 
						different purposes for your trip to China;
Â»Other materials related to the visa application, deemed 
						as necessary by the visa officer.
* For more information, please contact the local Chinese 
						Embassy in your country.
Invitation Letters
1. The invitation letter will be issued after the 
						successful registration.
2. Your name must be listed exactly as it appears on 
						your passport. Any differences between the name on your 
						passport and the name on your invitation letter or other 
						documentation could lead to a delay and/or denial of 
						your visa.
3. Please note ICCMS Conferences and the organizing 
						committee of the conference are not authorized to assist 
						with the VISA process beyond providing the Invitation 
						Letter issued by ICCMS Conferences and conference 
						Committee Board. Should your application be denied, 
						ICCMS Conferences and the organizing committee of the 
						conference cannot change the decision of the Ministry of 
						Foreign Affairs, nor will ICCMS Conferences and the 
						organizing committee of the conference engage in 
						discussion or correspondence with the MOFA or the 
						Embassy on behalf of the applicant. The registration fee 
						cannot be refunded when the VISA application of 
						individual being denied.
4. It takes around one month for the Embassy to process 
						the VISA application, please register as early as you 
						can to make sure you have enough time.

Keynote Speakers
Prof. Seifedine Kadry (IET Fellow)
Lebanese American University, USA
Seifedine Kadry is a 
									professor of Data Science at Lebanese 
									American university, USA. He holds a HdR 
									(Habilitation Degree) in Engineering 
									Sciences from Rouen University, France 
									(2017) and a Ph.D. in Engineering Sciences 
									from Blaise-Pascal University, France 
									(2007). He also earned an M.S. in Applied 
									Mathematics and Computer Science from EPFL 
									Lausanne, Switzerland (2002) and a B.S. in 
									Computer Science and Applied Mathematics 
									from the Lebanese University (1999). 
									Previously, he was a professor at the 
									Department of Mathematics and Computer 
									Science at Beirut Arab University and the 
									founder and director of the Data Science 
									Center there. He also held academic 
									positions at the American University of the 
									Middle East, Kuwait, and Lebanese 
									University, Lebanon.

The 15th International Conference on Computer Modeling 
						and Simulation (ICCMS 2023) was held in Dalian, China 
						during June 16-18, 2023. ICCMS 2023 was organized by 
						Dalian University of Technology, China, co-organized by 
						Chongqing University of Posts and Telecommunications, 
						China.
The speakers were Prof. 
						Gabriel Wainer, SCS Fellow, Carleton University, Canada; 
						Prof. Jian Sun, Tongji University, China; Prof. Xiong 
						Li, Sun Yat-sen University, China; and Associate 
						Research Fellow Qince Li, Harbin Institute of 
						Technology, China
ICCMS 2023 provided  a professional exposure and exchange 
						platform for academicians, researchers, scientists, 
						engineers and technologists internationally, who focus 
						on the field of Computer Modeling an aborate through 
						conference discussions.

Presenter Instruction
Instructions for Oral Presentations
âª Each speaker is allocated 15 minutes for your talk, 
						including the time for Q&A and for the changeover 
						between speakers, so please aim for no more than 12 
						minutes for the presentation itself. The slides and the 
						presentations must be in English.
âª In each oral presentation room, Laptop (with MS-Office 
						& Adobe Reader), projector & screen and laser pointer 
						will be available. You need to meet the chair of your 
						session 10 minutes before session start and test your 
						slides.
Instructions for Poster 
						Presentations
1. Poster Dimensions
Poster Size: A1 Size 23.4 inches (59.4 cm) wide x 33.1 
						inches (84.1 cm) high.
Please note that printing out your submitted full paper 
						in A4 size format is NOT acceptable.
2. Poster Content
Title: The conference short name, paper ID, and paper 
						title should appear at the top.
Text: Text should be readable from five feet away. Keep 
						the text brief. Try to use text to introduce the study, 
						explain visuals and direct viewers' attention to 
						significant data trends and relationships portrayed in 
						the visuals, state and explain the interpretations that 
						follow from the data.
Figures/ Tables: Each figure should have a brief title. 
						Try to use color figures rather than only black and 
						white text to make your poster attractive and highlight 
						the important technical content of your paper. Make sure 
						that the text and the visuals are integrated.
3. Poster Display
Author should post up your poster on the poster panel in 
						the morning of the day of your poster session.
Please note that your poster should be prepared well in 
						advance.
Please remove your poster before end of the day, or it 
						will be discarded.

Link: Discover Hangzhou
Title: 18th ICCMS 2026-Computer Modeling and Simulation 第十八届计算机建模与仿真国际会议
18th ICCMS 2026-Computer Modeling and Simulation 第十八届计算机建模与仿真国际会议
iccms_contact@163.com
+86-18302820449
Discover Hangzhou
Hangzhou, 
									the
																		capital of Zhejiang Province and Song 
									Dynasty capital, is known as 'Heaven on 
									Earth', with its stunning natural beauty and 
									rich cultural heritage. It was described by 
									Marco Polo as "the finest and most splendid 
									city in the world".
									Hangzhou is the political, economic, 
									cultural, educational, transportation, and 
									financial center of Zhejiang Province. The 
									2022 Asian Games was held in Hangzhou.
Where Hangzhou Is Located
Hangzhou is on the east coast of China, 
									south of the Yangtze River Delta and about 
									170 kilometers (105 miles) south of 
									Shanghai. By bullet train, Hangzhou is about 
									1 hour from Shanghai and 1.5 hours from 
									Suzhou.
The Features 
									of Hangzhou
▪
Hangzhou is
one of the Seven Ancient Capital Cities
, 
									with a history of about 2,500 years. Take a 
									cruise to explore the World Heritage Site - 
									the Grand Canal.
▪
Hangzhou boasts
enchanting natural scenery
.
West Lake
, the symbol of Hangzhou, is 
									considered to be one of the most beautiful 
									sights in China.
▪
Hangzhou offers
a variety of traditional cultures
, 
									such as tea, traditional Chinese medicine, 
									and Chinese silk.
▪
Hangzhou has a
highly developed transportation network
. 
									It is almost the only city in China 
									following 'pedestrian first' laws.
Hangzhou Is a 
									Leisurely City
Hangzhou is a special city. It is developing 
									at a high speed, while the lifestyle in 
									Hangzhou is leisurely. Compared to Shanghai, 
									the pace of life there is slow.
When traveling in Hangzhou, you can have a 
									stroll by West Lake, take a boat cruise, 
									feed the goldfish in the lake, and enjoy the 
									beautiful scenery. Then have a delicious 
									afternoon tea by the lake and chat with your 
									guide, learning about the story and history 
									of Hangzhou.
If you want to feel the classical charm of 
									Hangzhou, you can pay a visit to some water 
									towns. There are old buildings, boats, 
									rivers, ancient bridges, and wells. 
									Everything there is peaceful and beautiful.
If you want to know more about the tea 
									culture of Hangzhou, you can go to Meijiawu 
									Tea Plantation for an idyllic tour. Pick tea 
									leaves with a local tea farmer, watch a 
									Chinese tea ceremony, and learn about 
									Chinese tea culture.
5 Highlights 
									of Hangzhou
Hangzhou is a 
									perfect combination of history and 
									modernity, nature and culture. When you 
									visit Hangzhou, don't miss the following 
									highlights.
1. West Lake - the Legend of Hangzhou
West Lake is a symbol of Hangzhou. It is 
									famous for its stunning scenery and various 
									historical sites. It was selected as one of 
									the "12 superb sunset spots around the 
									world" by CNN.
West Lake
is gorgeous all year round. The spring 
									flowers, the summer lotuses, the autumn 
									moon, and the winter snow all make it very 
									charming.
2. Water Towns 
									Near Hangzhou
Wuzhen and Nanxun are the two most popular 
									water towns near Hangzhou.
Wuzhen
is one of the top six water towns in 
									China. Taking a gondola along the ancient 
									canal should not be missed and the nighttime 
									scenery in Wuzhen is impressive.
Nanxun
is not as famous as Wuzhen. It is thanks to 
									this that Nanxun is the most original and 
									picturesque town, filled with a tranquil 
									ambiance.
3. The Grand 
									Canal - World Heritage Site
The Grand Canal was dug out in 486 BC and 
									has a history of more than 2,500 years. It 
									runs from Beijing in the north to Hangzhou 
									in the south, stretching for 1,747 
									kilometers.
It played a great role in the economic and 
									cultural development, and with exchanges 
									between the north and south of China. It was 
									a great project created by the ancient 
									Chinese and is now one of the symbols of 
									Chinese culture.
4. Dragon Well Tea
Hangzhou's unique Dragon Well tea (Longjing 
									Tea) plays an important role among the 
									varieties of Chinese green tea. It is 
									produced mostly by hand, and is honored as 
									China's Famous Tea due to its high quality.
5. Hangzhou Silk
Hangzhou is regarded as the cradle of the 
									Chinese silk culture and production. With a 
									history of over 2,500 years, Hangzhou silk 
									is renowned for its gentle texture, gorgeous 
									colors, and wide varieties.
Convenient Transportation Network
Hangzhou is easily accessible by high-speed 
									trains and flights from main cities, such as 
									Beijing, Shanghai, Suzhou, Xi'an, Chengdu, 
									and Hong Kong.
From Hangzhou, the direct bus is the most 
									convenient way to get to nearby water towns. 
									A private tour with a private vehicle is the 
									best hassle-free option to avoid the traffic 
									congestion and help you maximize your time.
See more on
Hangzhou transportation
.
Emergency Phone 
									Numbers
▪ 110 for police
▪ 112 for traffic police (emergency only)
▪ 119 for fire services
▪ 120 for medical assistance or an ambulance
If you encounter a really complex situation, 
									call 110 first.

.

, including all figures, tables, and

Prof. Seifedine Kadry (IET Fellow)
Lebanese American University, USA
Seifedine Kadry is a 
									professor of Data Science at Lebanese 
									American university, USA. He holds a HdR 
									(Habilitation Degree) in Engineering 
									Sciences from Rouen University, France 
									(2017) and a Ph.D. in Engineering Sciences 
									from Blaise-Pascal University, France 
									(2007). He also earned an M.S. in Applied 
									Mathematics and Computer Science from EPFL 
									Lausanne, Switzerland (2002) and a B.S. in 
									Computer Science and Applied Mathematics 
									from the Lebanese University (1999). 
									Previously, he was a professor at the 
									Department of Mathematics and Computer 
									Science at Beirut Arab University and the 
									founder and director of the Data Science 
									Center there. He also held academic 
									positions at the American University of the 
									Middle East, Kuwait, and Lebanese 
									University, Lebanon.

ICCMS 2025
June 13-15, 2025 |
Zhuhai, China
The 17th International Conference on Computer Modeling 
						and Simulation (ICCMS 2025) was held in Zhuhai, China 
						during June 13-15, 2025 successfully. ICCMS 2025 is 
						co-organized by Intelligent IoT System Modeling and 
						Simulation Committee of China Simulation Federation, 
						China and Beihang University, China, co-hosted by Jinan 
						University (Zhuhai Campus), China; The Hong Kong 
						Polytechnic University, Hong Kong, China and Hangzhou 
						International Innovation Institute of Beihang 
						University, China. The conference featured 14 thematic 
						sub-forums covering hot topics such as intelligent 
						modeling and simulation, modeling and optimization 
						calculation in human-computer collaboration and 
						interactive design, Ssructural design, functional 
						simulation and simulation analysis in mechanical and 
						manufacturing systems.

Virtual Conference (Dalian, China)
The 16th International Conference on Computer Modeling and Simulation 
						(ICCMS 2024) was held virtually during 
						June 21-23, 2024. ICCMS 
						2024 was organized by Dalian University of Technology, 
						China, co-organized by Chongqing University of Posts and 
						Telecommunications, China and Heilongjiang Province Key 
						Laboratory of Laser Spectroscopy Technology and 
						Application, China.
Three keynote speakers and one invited speaker shared 
						with us their research works. They are Prof. Alejandro F 
						Frangi (IEEE Fellow & SPIE Fellow), The University of 
						Manchester, UK; Prof. Xiao Song, Beihang University, 
						China; Prof. Xiangjian He, University of Nottingham 
						Ningbo China, China.
ICCMS 2024 provided a professional exposure and exchange 
						platform for academicians, researchers, scientists, 
						engineers and technologists internationally, who focus 
						on the field of Computer Modeling and Simulation, to 
						share their researches and collaborate through 
						conference discussions.

						June 25-27, 2021. The conference had initially been 
						scheduled to be held in Melbourne, Australia, but due to 
						the outbreak of the COVID pandemic, the conference was 
						switched to the virtual conference form. ICCMS 2021 was 
						co-organized by Swinburne University of Technology, 
						Australia and University of Wollongong, Australia, and 
						technically supported by Chongqing University of Post 
						and Telecommunications, China.
Three renowned keynote speakers shared with us their 
						research works. They are Prof. Ming C. Lin (ACM Fellow, 
						IEEE Fellow) from University of Maryland, College Park, 
						USA; Prof. Makoto Iwasaki (IEEE Fellow) from Nagoya 
						Institute of Technology, Japan; Prof. Marimuthu 
						Palaniswami (IEEE Fellow) from The University of 
						Melbourne, Australia. Besides, there were three invited 
						speakers shared their latest research results with us.
Authors from Japan, Australia, Austria, The Netherlands, 
						China, etc. were divided into 7 parallel sessions. Their 
						presentations covered topics such as Computer and 
						Information Technology, Mechanical and Control 
						Engineering, Mathematical Model and Calculation, 
						Artificial Intelligence. All topics were interesting and 
						reflect the current focus of research and development in 
						the field.

Call for Papers
The 
									18th International Conference on Computer Modeling and Simulation (ICCMS 
									2026) is the premier forum for the presentation of 
						technological advances and research results in the 
						fields of Computer Modeling and Simulation. ICCMS 2026 
						will bring together leading engineers and scientists in 
									the field of Computer Modeling and Simulation from around the world.
Topics of interest for 
						submission include, but are not limited to:
Track 1: Fundamental Theories andMethodologies- Mathematical modeling and simulation techniques- Computational algorithms for simulation- Discrete-event simulation- Continuous and hybrid simulation models- Uncertainty quantification in simulationsTrack 2: Artificial Intelligence andMachine 
							Learning in SimulationTrack Chairs:Prof. Fei Guo, Chongqing 
							University of Posts and Telecommunications, ChinaAssoc. Prof. Zhongyong Zhao, Southwest University, 
							China- AI-driven modeling and simulation- Deep learning for predictive simulations- Reinforcement learning in dynamic systems- Neural networks for simulation optimization- Explainable AI in computational modelsTrack 3: High-Performance andLarge-Scale 
							Simulations- Parallel and distributed simulation- Cloud/edge computing for scalable simulations- GPU-accelerated modeling techniques- Big data analytics in simulation- Cyberinfrastructure for large-scale simulations | Track 4: Applications in 
							Engineering and Science- Computational fluid dynamics (CFD)- Structural and mechanical simulations- Climate and environmental modeling- Biomedical and healthcare simulations- Energy systems and smart grid simulationsTrack 5: Social, Economic, and Urban Systems 
							Simulation- Agent-based modeling for social systems- Economic forecasting and policy simulations- Urban planning and smart city simulations- Traffic and transportation modeling- Disaster response and crisis management 
							simulationsTrack 6: Emerging Technologies and Industry 4.0- Digital twins and virtual prototyping- IoT-enabled simulation systems- Robotics and autonomous systems simulation- Blockchain in simulation security

Reviewer Application
ICCMS conference 
						submissions are subject to a double-blind peer review 
						process by reviewers. Reviewers are members the 
						conference's Technical Committee of experts in fields 
						specifically matching the conference topics.
ICCMS 2026 welcomes competent scholars to join us as 
						reviewers. There are great benefits to becoming a 
						reviewer; including staying up-to-date with the latest 
						literature, advancing your career and establishing your 
						expertise and reputation in the field. ICCMS follows a 
						strict set of guidelines while accepting the applicant 
						as a reviewer. The minimum set of qualifications for 
						being a reviewer are listed below:
* Should be a holder of PhD degree from a recognized 
						university.
* Should be an author with a minimum of a 2 publications 
						in reputed journals or conferences.
If you would like to volunteer to become a reviewer for 
						ICCMS 2026 we recommend that you read more about the 
						peer review process. Peer review may seem like a 
						thankless task, but without it research would be 
						unreliable; ICCMS 2026 offer several incentives to 
						reward our reviewers.
To sign up as reviewer, please send your latest CV to 
						E-mail:
iccms_contact@163.com
